definitions.definition: A physical quantity that has only magnitude but no direction.
definitions.example: Mass and temperature are examples of scalar quantities.

definitions.definition: A physical quantity that has both magnitude and direction.
definitions.example: Force and displacement are examples of vector quantities.

definitions.definition: The length or size of a vector.
definitions.example: The magnitude of displacement is 10 units.

definitions.definition: The direction in which a vector is acting.
definitions.example: The direction of force is towards the right.

definitions.definition: A vector with a magnitude of 1 and any direction.
definitions.example: The unit vector along the x-axis is denoted by i.

definitions.definition: A vector that points from the origin to a point in a coordinate system.
definitions.example: The position vector of a point (a, b) is a vector of magnitude √(a^2 + b^2) with direction along the line joining the origin and the point.

definitions.definition: A vector with zero magnitude and any direction.
definitions.example: The zero vector is denoted by 0.

definitions.definition: Two vectors that have the same magnitude and direction.
definitions.example: Two vectors with the same magnitude and direction are equal.

definitions.definition: A vector whose magnitude is the same but direction is opposite to the given vector.
definitions.example: The negative of a vector is denoted by a dotted circle.

definitions.definition: The product of a scalar and a vector, which results in a new vector with the same direction as the original vector and a magnitude that is the product of the scalar and the original vector's magnitude.
definitions.example: The scalar product of 2 and a vector a is 2a.

definitions.definition: The process of combining two or more vectors to obtain a new vector.
definitions.example: The sum of two vectors a and b is a + b.

definitions.definition: A method of adding two vectors by forming a triangle with the two vectors and taking the third side as the resultant vector.
definitions.example: The sum of two vectors a and b is equal to the third side of the triangle formed by the two vectors.

definitions.definition: A method of adding two vectors by forming a parallelogram with the two vectors and taking the diagonal as the resultant vector.
definitions.example: The sum of two vectors a and b is equal to the diagonal of the parallelogram formed by the two vectors.

definitions.definition: The point that divides a line segment into two equal parts.
definitions.example: The midpoint of a line segment AB is given by (x1 + x2)/2, (y1 + y2)/2.

definitions.definition: The product of the magnitudes of two vectors and the cosine of the angle between them.
definitions.example: The dot product of two vectors a and b is given by a · b = |a| |b| cos(θ).

definitions.definition: The product of the magnitudes of two vectors and the sine of the angle between them, with the direction perpendicular to both vectors.
definitions.example: The cross product of two vectors a and b is given by a × b = |a| |b| sin(θ) k.

definitions.definition: The product of three scalars, which is equal to the volume of the parallelepiped formed by the three vectors.
definitions.example: The scalar triple product of three vectors a, b, and c is given by a · (b × c).
